title: Integration Gap Fixes
intention: Fix the seam gaps identified in the external code review that prevent the engine from functioning as a production durable workflow system. Each brief addresses a specific gap where components exist in isolation but are not wired together: crash recovery is stubbed, timers are in-memory-only, NIF contracts mismatch the SDK, the WebSocket route is unmounted, and continue_as_new is missing from the SDK.
problem: The replay machinery (command/history resolution, single-writer Recorder) exists but the production hook DeferredActiveWorkflowRecovery errors for any non-coordinator active workflow. aion-server never installs a real recovery seam, so a server restart with in-flight workflows fails to boot.

Timer components exist (timer wheel, TimerRecovery) but are not wired: sleep uses in-memory tokio::time::sleep, arm_timer is a no-op, and TimerRecovery is never constructed outside tests. A long-duration sleep blocks a thread and does not survive restart.

The Gleam SDK passes a closure to with_timeout but the Rust NIF decodes args[1] as a string. Both sides pass their own tests because each tests against its own fake — a cross-boundary contract mismatch.
WebSocket event streaming is fully implemented server-side and three client surfaces implement the client half, but the handler is never mounted on any HTTP route.
The subscription lifecycle example calls workflow.continue_as_new which does not exist in the aion_flow SDK. Without continue_as_new, long-running workflows accumulate unbounded event history.

solution.summary: Five targeted briefs, each wiring an existing component pair across a seam boundary. IG-001 implements the production ActiveWorkflowRecoverySeam and installs it in aion-server. IG-002 wires the timer wheel arming, timer recovery on startup, and durable sleep. IG-003 aligns the with_timeout NIF to accept a closure matching the Gleam SDK contract. IG-004 mounts the WebSocket subscription handler on the HTTP router. IG-005 adds the continue_as_new SDK binding, NIF, and engine handling.

decisions.title: Recovery uses existing AD replay infrastructure
decisions.choice: The production recovery seam delegates to the existing durability executor replay path rather than implementing a parallel recovery mechanism. This ensures recovery follows the same deterministic replay guarantees as normal operation.

decisions.title: Timers are durable by default
decisions.choice: All timer operations (sleep, with_timeout) record durability events and arm in the timer wheel. In-memory-only timers are eliminated. This is the only way to satisfy the durable execution contract for workflows with long sleeps.

decisions.title: NIF contracts match SDK signatures exactly
decisions.choice: The NIF argument decoding must match the Gleam SDK's published function signatures. When a mismatch is found, the NIF side is fixed to match the SDK, not the other way around, because the SDK is the author-facing contract.

decisions.title: WebSocket route follows existing API conventions
decisions.choice: The WebSocket handler is mounted alongside existing REST routes using the same middleware and authentication patterns. No special-casing.

decisions.title: continue_as_new terminates current run cleanly
decisions.choice: continue_as_new records a ContinueAsNew event, terminates the current process, and the engine starts a new run with fresh history. The new run uses the same workflow type and module version. This bounds event history growth for long-running workflows.

constraints.description: No modifications to the EventStore trait or conformance suite except where continue_as_new requires a new event variant.
The Recorder's single-writer invariant is never violated.
No backwards compatibility shims — replace, don't add alongside.
No arbitrary limits or hardcoded defaults for configurable values.
The determinism boundary is preserved: workflow.now remains the recorded event timestamp, no wall-clock time in workflow-visible paths.
Content-hash module namespacing invariant is preserved across continue_as_new.

goals: Server restart with pre-existing active workflows boots cleanly and resumes all in-flight workflows.
Timer-based sleeps are durable: they survive restart and do not block threads.
with_timeout NIF accepts a closure matching the Gleam SDK contract and executes it with a deadline.
WebSocket event streaming route is reachable by clients.
continue_as_new is available in the Gleam SDK and bounds event history growth for long-running workflows.
non_goals: No new persistence backends or store implementations.
No changes to the aion_flow SDK's public API beyond adding continue_as_new.
No new wire protocol or transport mechanisms.
No dashboard UI changes (the dashboard already implements the WebSocket client).
No changes to the .aion package format or content-hash versioning.

structure.crates/aion-server/src/state.rs: [IG-001] Install recovery seam in server engine builder
structure.crates/aion/src/engine/builder.rs: [IG-001, IG-002] Engine builder recovery and timer wiring
structure.crates/aion/src/runtime/nif_timer.rs: [IG-002, IG-003] Durable timer arming and with_timeout NIF fix
